hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 contacts, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric fit and reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack sufficient selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while maintaining key SSTR2 interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (particularly with SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing on these positions will allow us to design mutations that disrupt off-target contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues (FWKT at 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
Shifting the objective to prioritize selectivity (Δmargin) over binding affinity (ddG) will ensure that the design process actively seeks to improve SSTR2 selectivity over off-targets.
